title: Set up the auto-transfer to savings
subtitle: A single practical action for taking the willpower out of saving.
excerpt: A short activity from the Day 38 theme of the MONEY program. The task: set up one automatic transfer to savings today, even a small one, so the saving happens without a decision.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Automatic savings transfers as a money-management tool are a well-established personal finance practice.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you've committed to saving before and found it required more willpower than you had. The activity removes the decision from the equation.

content.text: A short activity from the Day 38 theme. Saving without willpower starts with removing the decision: if the transfer happens automatically, you never face the question of whether to do it.

Open your banking app or online banking. Set up one automatic transfer to a savings account — on the same day your pay or main income arrives. The amount does not have to be large. A small regular amount counts. The point is that it moves without you deciding.

If you already have a savings transfer running, increase it by one small amount: whatever you can do without strain. The goal is not an aggressive savings rate. It's making the habit automatic so it runs in the background.
Once it's set up, close the app. The decision is made. You don't need to think about it again until next month.
Some people feel mild resistance setting the number, as if the money is being taken away. If that comes up, pair this activity with the tapping practice for effortless savings.
Original to homemade.education. Automatic savings transfers as a money-management approach are a widely established personal finance practice.
